Fix: None - FRED data is always lagged 1+ days
Check: Agree. Make sure to delay everything by 2 days because of the FRED data reliance on other sources
TARS Response: AGREE. FRED data sources have publication delays: Treasury yields: published ~3:30 PM same day but not always in FRED same day. Credit spreads (IG, HY): 1-2 day lag in FRED. Gold/Oil: 1-2 day lag. DXY: 1 day lag. Will shift ALL FRED features by 2 days to be safe. This means for a Monday trade, we use Thursdays FRED data.

Check: Is this using the past 5 closing for vol or using minute vol as well?
TARS Response: rvol_5m uses log returns of 1-minute CLOSE prices over the trailing 5 bars. Formula: std(log(close_i / close_i-1)) * sqrt(390*252). Uses close-to-close returns, not OHLC. This is standard for realized vol from tick data. SAFE - all from live 1-min bars.

Check: Is this using the past 10 closing for vol or using minute vol as well?
TARS Response: rvol_10m uses log returns of 1-minute CLOSE prices over the trailing 10 bars. Formula: std(log(close_i / close_i-1)) * sqrt(390*252). Uses close-to-close returns, not OHLC. This is standard for realized vol from tick data. SAFE - all from live 1-min bars.

Check: Is this using the past 15 closing for vol or using minute vol as well?
TARS Response: rvol_15m uses log returns of 1-minute CLOSE prices over the trailing 15 bars. Formula: std(log(close_i / close_i-1)) * sqrt(390*252). Uses close-to-close returns, not OHLC. This is standard for realized vol from tick data. SAFE - all from live 1-min bars.

Check: Is this using the past 30 closing for vol or using minute vol as well?
TARS Response: rvol_30m uses log returns of 1-minute CLOSE prices over the trailing 30 bars. Formula: std(log(close_i / close_i-1)) * sqrt(390*252). Uses close-to-close returns, not OHLC. This is standard for realized vol from tick data. SAFE - all from live 1-min bars.

Check: Is this using the past 30 closing for calc or using minute vol as well?
TARS Response: Garman-Klass uses OHLC from each 1-min bar over 30 bars. Formula: 0.5*ln(H/L)^2 - (2ln2-1)*ln(C/O)^2, averaged over 30 bars. Uses open, high, low, close of each minute bar. More efficient than close-to-close. SAFE - all from live 1-min bars.
